2605.01189.title: NEURON: A Neuro-symbolic System for Grounded Clinical Explainability
2605.01189.paper_summary: Clinical AI adoption is hindered by the black-box/grey-box nature of high-performing models, which lack the ontological grounding and narrative transparency required for professional-level explainability. We present NEURON, a neuro-symbolic system designed to enhance both predictive reliability and clinical interpretability. NEURON integrates SNOMED CT ontology-informed structural representations with machine learning models to bridge the gap between raw data and medical nomenclature. To facilitate human-aligned interaction, the system utilizes a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) grounded LLM layer to synthesize SHAP feature attributions and patient-specific clinical notes into coherent, natural-language explanations. Validated on the MIMIC-IV dataset for Acute Heart Failure mortality prediction, NEURON improved the AUC from 0.74-0.77 to 0.84-0.88 and significantly outperformed raw SHAP visualizations in human-aligned metrics (0.85 vs. 0.50). Our results demonstrate that NEURON offers a robust, scalable engineering solution for deploying trustworthy, human-centered connected health applications.
2605.01189.paper_summary_zh: 臨床AI的採用受到高效能模型的黑箱/灰箱特性所阻礙，這些模型缺乏專業級解釋所需的本體基礎和敘事透明度。我們提出了NEURON，一個旨在增強預測可靠性和臨床可解釋性的神經符號系統。NEURON將基於SNOMED CT本體的結構表示與機器學習模型整合，以彌合原始數據與醫學命名法之間的鴻溝。為了促進與人類的對齊互動，該系統利用檢索增強生成（RAG）基礎的LLM層，將SHAP特徵歸因和患者特定的臨床筆記合成為連貫的自然語言解釋。在MIMIC-IV數據集上針對急性心衰竭死亡率預測進行驗證，NEURON將AUC從0.74-0.77提升至0.84-0.88，並在與人類對齊的指標中顯著超越原始SHAP可視化（0.85對0.50）。我們的結果表明，NEURON提供了一個穩健、可擴展的工程解決方案，用於部署值得信賴的以人為本的連接健康應用。
2605.01189.author: Anuradha Chandrasekaran et.al.
2605.01189.authors: Anuradha Chandrasekaran, Dimitrios Zikos, Mutlu Mete, Alan Pang, Brady D. Lund, Kewei Sha
